_meta.purpose: The content-driven selection layer. Each section maps a CONTENT SHAPE / INTENT to a concrete section pattern. To compose a page: split the prompt into content groups, match each group to the section whose `whenToUse` fits best, then order them per template.json. This replaces 'copy one example' and is what makes layouts differ by content instead of by chance.
_meta.usage: Pick, do not invent. If a content group matches no section, STOP and flag for human review (never approximate). Fill every placeholder with real content; drop a node only when no content applies.
_meta.patternSource: Each `pattern` id resolves in landingpage/block-patterns.json; each block id in landingpage/blocks.json.

hero.intent: Open the page: the core promise + the primary action.
hero.whenToUse: Always, once, as the first section. The single most important message + the page's primary CTA (Brand), optionally a Filled secondary.

feature-grid.intent: Present a set of EQUIVALENT items that each have (or suit) an image — features, destinations, product highlights.
feature-grid.whenToUse: 3–6 equal items where each benefits from a visual (e.g. Reiseziele, Produkt-Features mit Bild). Each item = image-teaser (image + heading + short text + Link). Use card teasers instead only when there is no meaningful imagery (→ card-grid).

card-grid.intent: Present a set of EQUIVALENT options WITHOUT strong imagery — fare tiers, help topics, resources, FAQ entries.
card-grid.whenToUse: 3–6 equal, text-first options (e.g. Tickets/Tarife, Service-Themen, Ressourcen). Each item = a Card with heading + description + Link. Prefer this over feature-grid when images would be filler.
card-grid.note: Same grid pattern as feature-grid but composed from card teasers (no image) instead of image teasers.

media-text-rows.intent: Explain a sequence / story / how-it-works where each point needs a paragraph + a supporting image.
media-text-rows.whenToUse: 2–4 explanatory points that are NOT interchangeable list items — a process, benefits that need context, before/after. Alternate image side row to row for rhythm.

highlight.intent: Spotlight ONE standout item — a launch, a featured offer, a key announcement.
highlight.whenToUse: Exactly one thing to emphasize between grids/rows. A single wide Card with image + text + Link. Never use for a set of equal items.

cta-centered.intent: Close the page with a single conversion action.
cta-centered.whenToUse: Always, once, as the last section. Centered heading + a single Brand button (or Brand + Filled group).
